reforms.description: The VCT and EIS provide tax incentives for investments in smaller firms, while the Challenge Fund initiative encourages partnerships between public, private, and community sectors to bid for public funds aimed at local service development and regeneration, both aimed at stimulating growth and innovation in the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to stimulate investment and innovation in smaller firms, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The VCT and EIS are operational, and the Challenge Fund has been utilized for public funding bids.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these reforms are significant in promoting innovation, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The VCT and EIS both offer tax incentives for investment in smaller firms in the form of capital gains and income tax reliefs for investors.

reforms.description: The Private Finance Initiative (PFI) was introduced to shift the public sector's role from direct service provision to becoming a procurer and regulator, aiming to improve efficiency and value for money through private sector involvement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing efficiency and value for money, the PFI can lead to better public services and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The PFI has been operational since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public sector financing and service delivery.
reforms.source_quote: The central idea underlying PFI is a fundamental shift in the role of the public sector from being a direct provider of service towards becoming a procurer and a regulator for services that were once publicly provided.

reforms.description: The UK government has shifted its approach to science and technology policy, focusing on improving framework conditions for innovation and technology diffusion rather than direct grants, with an emphasis on facilitating industry exploitation of public spending on R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This shift aims to enhance the effectiveness of public R&D spending and promote innovation in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly stated this new approach, but specific implementation details are pending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a fundamental change in the government's approach to supporting innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The UK government's funding for civil research and development is below but not dissimilar to that of other countries...reflects a shift in the United Kingdom's overall approach to science and technology policy towards improving the framework conditions for innovation and technology diffusion.

reforms.description: The SMART programme was launched to stimulate high-technology projects in small firms, providing grant-based assistance to promote innovative activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support for high-technology projects, the SMART programme encourages innovation and can lead to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The SMART programme has been operational since its launch.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the programme is significant, it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: SMART (Small Firms Merit Award for Research and Technology) was launched in April 1986 to stimulate high-technology projects in small firms.

reforms.description: The Business Start-up scheme replaced the Enterprise Allowance Scheme in 1991, aimed at supporting new business creation and reducing unemployment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating new business creation, the scheme contributes to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Business Start-up scheme has been operational since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant initiative to support entrepreneurship and reduce unemployment.
reforms.source_quote: The Enterprise Allowance Scheme (EAS) was launched in 1983 and operated until its replacement in 1991 by the Business Start-up scheme.

reforms.description: The Teaching Company Scheme promotes technology transfer to small firms by placing graduates in SMEs for two-year periods.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the placement of graduates in SMEs, the scheme enhances knowledge transfer and supports innovation in small firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The Teaching Company Scheme has been operational and placing graduates in SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the scheme is important for knowledge transfer, it does not represent a major overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Teaching Company Scheme promotes technology transfer to small firms by placing graduates in SMEs for two-year periods.
